abstract.text: Sync Core Data stores across devices using event sourcing.

inlineContent.text: Ensembles synchronizes Core Data persistent stores by recording local saves as events, exchanging them through a pluggable cloud backend, and replaying them on each peer to reach eventual consistency.
An
monitors a single persistent store. When the app saves, Ensembles captures the changes automatically. Calling
downloads remote events, replays them into the local store, and uploads new local events. Conflicts are resolved through causal revision tracking, with delegate hooks for custom repair logic.
The simplest way to add sync is with
, which creates the Core Data stack, handles the delegate, and auto-syncs on save:

code: // That's it — auto-sync handles attach, sync, and ongoing saves

inlineContent.text: For more control, use
directly:
Ensembles ships with several cloud backends:
CloudKitFileSystem
— Syncs via iCloud using CloudKit.
GoogleDriveCloudFileSystem
— Syncs via Google Drive REST API v3.
OneDriveCloudFileSystem
— Syncs via Microsoft OneDrive (Graph API v1.0).
PCloudCloudFileSystem
— Syncs via pCloud, a Swiss provider with strong privacy guarantees.
WebDAVCloudFileSystem
— Syncs via any WebDAV server (Nextcloud, ownCloud, etc.).
EncryptedCloudFileSystem
— Wraps any backend with AES-256-GCM encryption.
LocalCloudFileSystem
— Uses a local directory, useful for testing or shared-folder sync.
MemoryCloudFileSystem
— An in-memory actor-based backend for unit testing.
Any storage that can hold files at paths can serve as a backend. See
for a guide to implementing your own.
Ensembles 3 reads and writes Ensembles 2 cloud data and event store formats. Existing apps can migrate without a data reset. See
for a step-by-step guide.
